title: Add A Fold-Safe Latent 'Remaining Stops' Strategy State
summary: Heilmeier’s VSE reports that remaining pit stops materially improves pit-call quality, and their pit probabilities differ by intended stop count.
rationale: Heilmeier’s VSE reports that remaining pit stops materially improves pit-call quality, and their pit probabilities differ by intended stop count. This is adjacent to but not the same as prior time-to-pit work: the useful state is not exact horizon, but the coarse strategic template still left in the race.
implementation_hint: Create an auxiliary multiclass target per row such as future tire-change stops remaining in {0,1,2,3+}, computed within each driver-race on train only. Train an OOF auxiliary model with race-grouped folds, then feed the OOF class probabilities and argmax into the main AUC model. Test especially on dry laps, with a separate fallback for wet/intermediate compounds.
expected_effect: Should help separate late-stint 'must stop soon' rows from long one-stop rows that currently look similar on raw wear features.
risk: If the auxiliary target is too correlated with fold leakage or too noisy in wet/testing sessions, it can simply repackage the current signal without adding generalization.
